title: Analytics - Settings Changed
scenario: Firebase analytics "setting_changed" event logging does not display device PID, global setting ID, and settings value after changing device settings in the Poly Studio Mobile app
steps: Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the mobile device.
Navigate to the Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
Tap on the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Make a change to a device setting (e.g., toggle ANC, adjust Equalizer, or change Transparency Mode).
Open a web browser and navigate to the Firebase Analytics console for the app.
Locate and open the analytics page for the "setting_changed" event.
Verify that the page displays the following default elements: graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment.
Verify that the event details include: device PID, global setting ID, and settings value.
Wait for some time (e.g., 5–10 minutes) for analytics data to propagate.
Refresh the Firebase Analytics page and check the event count for related elements (e.g., the specific setting changed).
Observe whether the event count has increased after the setting change.
expected: The Firebase Analytics \"setting_changed\" event page opens properly and displays all default elements (graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment) along with device PID, global setting ID, and settings value. After changing a device setting, the event count for related elements increases after some time.
